title: Extract a deep module owning the Copilot ChatCompletions wire primitives shared by both translators

body.summary: The Anthropic translator (src/routes/messages/) and the Codex/Responses translator (src/routes/responses/) both convert client requests into the SAME upstream ChatCompletionsPayload, and several transformations are duplicated near-verbatim. There is no neutral module that owns 'talking to the Copilot ChatCompletions shape', so each primitive is implemented (and must be fixed) twice.
body.problem: Duplicated transformations into the shared ChatCompletionsPayload: (1) thinking-budget resolution -- resolveThinkingBudget at non-stream-translation.ts:76-83 vs request-translation.ts:96-104; the keyword/anchor floor combine at non-stream:41-49 vs request:152-160; the 'thinking-on -> drop temperature/top_p, spread thinking_budget' block at non-stream:63-68 vs request:175-180 (effectively identical). (2) Tool lowering -- translateAnthropicToolsToOpenAI at non-stream:342-356 vs the 'function' case of lowerTools at request:242-257 (same target {type:'function',function:{name,description,parameters}}). (3) Content-part lowering -- mapContent at non-stream:290-340 vs mapResponsesContent at request:562-587 (identical hasImage gate -> join('\n\n') else build ContentPart[] with image_url). (4) Usage reshaping (input_tokens = prompt_tokens - cached_tokens) appears 3x in messages/ (non-stream:426-436, stream:44-54, stream:161-171) and once in responses/ (responses stream:129-140). The seam is already half-formed: detectKeywordBudget is shared, but responses/request-translation.ts:9 imports it by reaching ACROSS into the other translator's file (../messages/non-stream-translation) -- that cross-route import is the tell that the primitive has no neutral home.
body.solution: Introduce one deep module (e.g. src/services/copilot/wire/ or src/lib/copilot-wire.ts) that owns the Copilot ChatCompletions primitives: thinking-budget resolution (resolveThinkingBudget + the temp/top_p drop rule), function-tool lowering, content-part lowering (text/image), and usage reshaping (cached-token math). Both translators become thin adapters mapping their CLIENT vocabulary (Anthropic blocks / Responses items) onto these neutral primitives. Move the shared detectKeywordBudget into this module too so the cross-route import disappears.
body.benefits: Locality: the budget < max_tokens-1 invariant, the temperature/top_p drop rule, and the cached-token subtraction each live in ONE place instead of being fixed in 2-4. Leverage: a fourth wire format starts from primitives instead of copy-paste. Tests: the invariants get a single test surface instead of being re-tested per translator. The cross-file import smell (responses reaching into messages) is removed.
body.deletion_test: Deleting this proposed module would re-scatter the same 4 transformations back across both translators -- complexity reappears across callers, so the module earns its keep.
acceptance_criteria: resolveThinkingBudget exists exactly once and both translators call it
The temperature/top_p drop + thinking_budget spread rule exists exactly once
Usage reshaping (cached-token math) exists exactly once
bun test passes; bun run typecheck passes; bun run lint passes
out_of_scope: tool_choice translation (genuinely different between the two -- responses resolves synthetic/allowed_tools); leave per-translator
Streaming SSE state machine (tracked separately in ARCH-002)

progress.note: Task created from improve-codebase-architecture review. Duplication verified by reading both translator bodies (non-stream-translation.ts, request-translation.ts) and the three usage sites in messages/stream-translation.ts.
